## What ?

Chorus turns coding tasks into live cards on a spatial canvas where humans manage agents through an automated, Kanban-style workflow.

## What but better ?

Chorus is a collaborative workspace that sits between you and AI coding agents. Instead of chatting back and forth in a thread, you get a visual board where every task is a card that moves through stages—queued, in progress, needs approval, done. Agents work on the cards, you control the flow. Fun part is you can work on multipe projects at once, and see all your agents' work in one place.

## What pain points does this solve?

**Context fragmentation.** You lose track of what the agent is doing across long sessions. Chorus keeps every task visible and stateful.

**Surprise changes.** Agents go off-script and modify things you didn't ask for. Chorus adds approval gates before destructive operations.

**No oversight.** You cannot manage multiple agents working in parallel. Chorus gives you a dashboard to route, pause, and redirect tasks across agents.

**Ephemeral history.** Chat threads disappear or get too long to parse. Chorus persists task state, decisions, and outcomes on a canvas you can revisit.

**Coordination overhead.** Running a hackathon team with AI help is chaotic. Chorus structures agent work into a workflow humans can actually follow.
